title: lathyrus pratensis subsp. lusseri
intro: Lathyrus pratensis subsp. lusseri, also known as the meadow peavine or grass vetchling, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, with medicinal properties attributed to its flowers, leaves, and stems. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and inflammatory conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Lathyrus pratensis subsp. lusseri can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
